title: Hatcliffe Top Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 4 killed; pilot Flg Off. Robert Laval Proudfoot

external_narratives.summary: The IWM register lists this memorial as commemorating Lancaster JB596 of 100 Squadron. The crash occurred on 17 December 1943, part of the notorious 'Black Thursday' Berlin raid when thick fog caused heavy losses among returning crews.
key_details: Part of Black Thursday losses 16-17 December 1943
25 Lancasters lost on Berlin operation plus 31 more on return due to fog

external_narratives.summary: Photographic record of the memorial stone at Hatcliffe Top showing it incorporates a piece of the aircraft's skin and relics from the crash. The Lancaster hit a tree close to the memorial site and crashed into the pine trees behind.
key_details: Aircraft hit treetop before crashing into pine plantation

external_narratives.summary: CWGC records confirm crew details for the Black Thursday losses. The pilot Flg Off. Robert Laval Proudfoot was aged 24. Four crew were killed (Proudfoot, Sgt Sidney David Viggers aged 19, Sgt John Bamford aged 23, Sgt Bernard Heaton aged 21) while three crew survived with serious injuries. A farm worker was severely burned rescuing survivors from the blaze.
key_details: Three crew survived with serious injuries
Farm worker burned during rescue attempt
